commitments.expected_result: results or a no-card receipt come only from the validated current active index
commitments.failure_boundary: a missing, stale, or invalid index returns an explicit unavailable error and never scans another authority
commitments.label: Serve predictive retrieval only from the validated current index
commitments.rationale: The active index is the sole retrieval authority; an alternate reader would recreate stale eligibility risk. Distinct cards in one exact generation and authority scope reuse the same immutable mesh view so large local brains remain within the declared runtime budget.
commitments.validation_boundary: FlowGuard lifecycle model, same-class distinct-card mesh-reuse test, independent benchmark-instrumentation test, real current-generation runtime benchmark, and aggregate readiness

commitments.expected_result: one exact PID/thread/token owner performs the mutation; live competing owners are not displaced; the active owner thread may safely reenter; dead or interrupted owners are recovered
commitments.failure_boundary: a live owner remains authoritative, while an unrecoverable acquisition or release failure blocks Sleep, Dream, postflight, and index completion visibly
commitments.label: Serialize lifecycle mutation through one recoverable identified writer
preconditions: the lifecycle authority root is current
commitments.rationale: Lifecycle mutation and active-index activation need one shared ownership contract; directory existence alone cannot distinguish a live writer from interrupted state or prove terminal release.
commitments.trigger: mutates lifecycle state or activates a rebuilt index generation
commitments.validation_boundary: FlowGuard lifecycle known-bad proofs, focused lock protocol tests, real Sleep/Dream receipts, and aggregate readiness

commitments.expected_result: Sleep publishes the model at most once, persists one same-state calibration snapshot for each new parked evidence digest, gives final index validation to one owner, settles pending Dream handoff observations through the same bounded atomic lifecycle batch, replays lifecycle duplicate membership in linear event-count work, and acknowledges handoffs only after model publication commits
commitments.failure_boundary: a duplicate model publication, missing calibration watermark, calibration-only eligibility invalidation, per-handoff full lifecycle replay, quadratic duplicate-key work inside one replay, or handoff acknowledgement before model commit blocks convergence
commitments.label: Converge no-delta Sleep through one model publication and one evidence watermark
preconditions: current model and lifecycle authorities are readable
commitments.rationale: A no-delta model identity and a same-state evidence checkpoint are different responsibilities; neither licenses repeating the complete publication pipeline.
state_writes: active index when retrieval eligibility is stale
commitments.trigger: the current model is unchanged or parked evidence changes without satisfying reopening
commitments.validation_boundary: FlowGuard known-bad proofs, focused Sleep tests, real no-delta receipt, and aggregate readiness

commitments.expected_result: organization cards are read from kb/main, incoming material is read from kb/imports, and every distinct maintenance decision has one stable unique id with an exact count
commitments.failure_boundary: old trusted/candidates roots or an old manifest block runtime use and are handled only by the upgrade migrator
commitments.label: Consume organization knowledge only from the current main/imports schema
preconditions: the organization mirror has completed direct canonical migration
commitments.rationale: An old organization layout is migration input, never a second daily authority.
commitments.trigger: connects, validates, downloads, or maintains an organization KB
commitments.validation_boundary: organization schema migration, current-layout validation, and scheduled production receipt

commitments.expected_result: the strongest current declared model and deepest supported reasoning effort are written into the automation
commitments.failure_boundary: missing current provider/config authority blocks installation instead of selecting a fixed model or effort
commitments.label: Resolve scheduled automation runtime only from current provider facts
commitments.rationale: A fixed backup model is an undeclared authority and can silently weaken later machines.
commitments.trigger: installs or refreshes a retained automation
commitments.validation_boundary: runtime resolution tests, target-owned install checks, and clean consumer projection

commitments.expected_result: each maintenance unit validates its own performed or not-applicable obligations from exact target-native fields and writes one immutable receipt for its own run
commitments.failure_boundary: missing or forged target-native evidence, nonexistent branch fields, generic shared no-op evidence, cross-unit receipt reuse, or author-control runtime dependence blocks completion
commitments.label: Close each scheduled automation only from its own proof-bound native receipt
preconditions: the selected target skill and its native entrypoint are current
the exact target-owned run id and command identity are frozen before native execution
commitments.rationale: A skill proves its own work. Author-side depth certification remains separate and no other skill may reuse this run as completion evidence.
commitments.trigger: receives one immutable native automation receipt
commitments.validation_boundary: FlowGuard known-bad cases, distinct target-native receipt tests, source-only author contract audit, and aggregate readiness

commitments.expected_result: the upgrader inventories old material, writes the current form once, removes old authority, and proves residual count zero
commitments.failure_boundary: unmigrated, ambiguous, or concurrently changed input rolls back or remains paused and cannot enter normal runtime
commitments.label: Upgrade old Chaos Brain state directly to one canonical current state
preconditions: all four retained background automations are transaction-paused
commitments.rationale: Old shapes are bounded migration input; successful normal operation has exactly one current path.
commitments.trigger: finds an older managed schema, state, route, or installation surface
commitments.validation_boundary: versioned migration fixtures, semantic anti-downgrade positive/negative regressions, residual scan, FlowGuard known-bad cases, and aggregate readiness

commitments.expected_result: the visible status distinguishes current, fast-forward available, local ahead, diverged, and unavailable for the exact configured upstream
commitments.failure_boundary: a missing upstream or failed comparison is shown as unavailable and never as current or actionable
commitments.label: Show configured-upstream update status without an update action
commitments.rationale: Visibility is a product behavior but must not carry software-mutation authority.
commitments.trigger: opens the Khaos Brain desktop application
commitments.validation_boundary: topology unit tests, desktop thread/interaction tests, conformance replay, and live UI click-through

commitments.expected_result: one explicit invocation may enter the existing transactional fast-forward update route after every safety gate passes
commitments.failure_boundary: missing invocation authorization, UI-open, dirty, ahead, diverged, missing-upstream, or failed validation blocks before unsafe mutation
commitments.label: Apply software updates only through the explicit manual route

path_authority.business_intent: Apply one explicitly requested Khaos Brain software update

preconditions: explicit user request is present in the current invocation
configured upstream is a clean fast-forward target
commitments.rationale: A single invocation-scoped route replaces persisted or scheduled authorization without weakening update safety.
side_effects: restore four surviving automations after closure
commitments.trigger: receives an invocation-scoped explicit user-request flag
commitments.validation_boundary: FlowGuard update gate, native receipt tests, clean installer checks, and fast-forward/rollback/restoration evidence

commitments.expected_result: AI invokes the one manual product path only after the user explicitly asks for an update in the current conversation
commitments.failure_boundary: unclear, inferred, historical, persisted, or scheduled intent cannot be promoted into current invocation authority
commitments.label: Invoke the manual updater only for an explicit current user request

path_authority.business_intent: Invoke the manual updater for the user's explicit current request

preconditions: the user explicitly requests the update in the current conversation
commitments.rationale: Agent intent is distinct from product execution and may only invoke, not absorb, the product runtime contract.

relations.rationale: The agent route supplies invocation-scoped intent to the separately owned product runtime path.

commitments.trigger: the current user message explicitly asks AI to update Khaos Brain
commitments.validation_boundary: Skill activation wording, target contract inventory, missing-request negative test, and guarded invocation test

commitments.expected_result: fresh install, upgrade, repair, and repeat install retain four background automations, keep the manual skill, and keep the exact old task absent
commitments.failure_boundary: any exact retired-task residual, recreation, survivor-state loss, or similarly named user-asset mutation blocks installation confidence
commitments.label: Keep the automatic system-update task exactly retired
commitments.rationale: Task retirement is an installer/upgrade responsibility and cannot be delegated to UI or the manual update skill.

relations.rationale: The development process validates that the product update path remains manual after installation.

commitments.trigger: installs, upgrades, repairs, or checks the managed Khaos Brain runtime
commitments.validation_boundary: fresh/upgrade/repeat installer tests, exact-name negative tests, convergence model, real install, and independent install check

commitments.actor: retired Khaos Brain system update automation
commitments.expected_result: no automatic software update is performed
commitments.failure_boundary: any scheduler, UI-prepared authorization, or automatic retry residual is a failed retirement
commitments.label: Automatically update Khaos Brain
commitments.rationale: The user explicitly removed delegated automatic software-update authority and retained only visibility plus a manual AI route.
commitments.scoped_out_reason: Automatic software update behavior is intentionally removed; read-only status and explicit manual update are separate commitments.
commitments.validation_boundary: exact scheduler absence, no UI authorization, and no automatic update entrypoint

ledger.project_boundary: Chaos Brain current runtime and direct upgrade migration
ledger.rationale: Register the sole current success paths before removing every compatibility and fallback surface.

source_surfaces.rationale: Canonical machine retrieval command.

source_surfaces.rationale: Normative retrieval behavior.

source_surfaces.rationale: Sole current owner-aware serialization implementation for lifecycle mutation.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: owner recovery, reentrancy, live-owner, and release-failure tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Index activation delegates to the same lifecycle writer authority rather than introducing a second lock contract.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: active-index publication and Sleep final-owner tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Normative owner, recovery, reentrancy, and terminal-release behavior.

source_surfaces.rationale: Owns the distinction between final model identity and final index validation.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: single publication and final index-owner tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Persists a new evidence digest once without changing retrieval eligibility.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: same-state calibration snapshot tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Acknowledgement is terminal only after the handoff-derived model authority commits.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: publication-failure, dead-owner recovery, and exact acknowledgement tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Typed Dream handoffs are Sleep-owned observations and must share the same bounded admission/disposition batch instead of replaying the full ledger per item.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: multi-handoff atomic-batch regression plus per-item-replay known-bad rejection

source_surfaces.rationale: The ordered durable idempotency projection is not the membership index; duplicate validation must use one in-memory set lookup per lifecycle event.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: linear-scaling regression, real-corpus replay timing, and quadratic known-bad rejection

source_surfaces.rationale: Normative no-delta publication and final-index ownership boundary.

source_surfaces.validation_boundary: strict OpenSpec validation

source_surfaces.rationale: Clone/fetch, direct migration, and current validation entrypoint.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: organization source tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Sole runtime schema gate.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: organization schema tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Scheduled current-layout consumer.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: organization maintenance tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Central current-layout reader blocks every obsolete organization root before serving cards.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: multi-source current-layout and obsolete-root rejection tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Contribution dedupe reads only main/imports through the central current-layout reader.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: organization outbox tests and current-runtime static gate

source_surfaces.rationale: Adoption consumes only current organization cards and commits the resulting local active index.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: organization adoption and active-index commit tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Repository automation rejects obsolete roots instead of validating an alternate layout.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: installed organization GitHub checker tests and current-runtime static gate

source_surfaces.rationale: Distinct merge, split, and other maintenance actions must never collapse onto one receipt identity.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: organization maintenance decision-id uniqueness and native receipt tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Sole runtime policy resolver.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: runtime resolution tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Writes resolved current policy into retained tasks.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: transactional install tests

source_surfaces.rationale: The native owner is the sole authority for performed, not-applicable, and failed obligation evidence.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: native receipt disposition and branch-source tests

source_surfaces.rationale: The target-native receipt validator verifies exact facts and never expands catalog entries into synthetic success.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: exact target payload projection, applicability proof, phase universe, and immutable receipt tests

source_surfaces.rationale: The author-side generator binds one skill to one maintenance unit, unique obligations, and its own depth calibration.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: deterministic source-only generation, author compiler parity, unique evidence ownership, and clean consumer projection

source_surfaces.rationale: Terminal construction consumes only the exact target-owned native result and cannot depend on another skill or author-side runtime.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: same-run command, payload, obligation evidence, immutable hash, and no cross-unit receipt reuse

source_surfaces.rationale: The consumer projection excludes author-control files and the scheduler launches only the selected target's native runner with one exact run identity.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: clean consumer manifest, no author-control tokens, target-native entrypoint, and FlowGuard consumer-independence known-bad proofs

source_surfaces.rationale: Cross-machine upgrade entrypoint.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: upgrade fixture matrix

source_surfaces.rationale: Current-to-current capability is compared through obligation, evidence, and mandatory-owner coverage, not through obsolete check-name, route, or depth-dimension subset preservation.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: coverage-preserving check and conditional-owner reorganization positive tests plus lost-obligation/evidence/owner negative tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Direct-to-current data migration owner.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: migration receipts and residual scans

source_surfaces.rationale: Moves exact obsolete failure state out of the daily wrapper.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: update-state migration tests

source_surfaces.rationale: The aggregate production gate may only observe current update state; any later failure restores the exact prior bytes.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: pre-assurance migration-order and exact rollback-byte tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Each managed source skill is compiled into a clean consumer projection; the installed tree must equal that projection and contain no author-control files or runtime dependency.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: clean consumer manifest, source-to-installed projection parity, no author-control token leakage, and target-native currentness checks

source_surfaces.rationale: Installs one explicit command grammar without old argument aliases.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: launcher CLI tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Requires one explicit source or release runtime and rejects path probing or automatic switching.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: desktop launcher current-runtime tests and static residual gate

source_surfaces.rationale: Moves maintainer aliases into the current organization-maintenance settings once and removes normal alias reads/writes.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: desktop settings direct-migration and strict-reader tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Collapses four retired Skill-guidance aliases into one current field outside daily card readers.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: card guidance direct-migration and current-reader tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Strict configured-upstream topology inspector and status-only cache owner.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: topology, missing-upstream, fetch-failure, and schema-v2 tests

source_surfaces.rationale: Read-only launch-time background status projection with no hitbox or mutation callback.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: desktop background-worker tests and live pointer click-through

source_surfaces.rationale: Sole native product-runtime entrypoint for one invocation-scoped manual update.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: explicit-request, UI-open, fast-forward, dirty-tree, rollback, and terminal native-receipt tests

source_surfaces.rationale: The native receipt is written by the same manual product path and cannot create a second updater.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: manual trigger identity, target-native checks, exact restoration, final installed-health readback, CURRENT, cleanup, and receipt integrity

source_surfaces.rationale: Agent-operation activation boundary supplies intent to the product path only for an explicit current request.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: activation-boundary review, source-only author contract integrity, and missing-request rejection

source_surfaces.rationale: Installer-owned exact retirement, four-survivor state preservation, and manual-skill retention.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: fresh, upgrade, missing-manifest, similar-name, repeat-install, and real install-check evidence

source_surfaces.rationale: Normative retirement, manual-skill retention, and repeated-install behavior.
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: OpenSpec 1.6.0 strict validation
